title: EPOMAKER Macaron 1.8m Coiled Type-C to USB A TPU Mechanical Keyboard Cable with Detachable Aviator Connector for Gaming Keyboard/Tablet/Smart Phone (Purple)
features: 【Epomaker Macaron Cable For Keyboards and More】 To provide more options of cables, Epomaker custom Macaron cable is designed to match your keyboard, no matter what type of setup you’re working with. Chose from our different color options, it comes easy for you to match your keyboard and top off your setup. Besides that, this cable features all you want and need: Double-shielded USB 2.0 compliant, 61-gauge, 4-conductor, etc.

【Durable and Flexible】Compared with EPOMAKER MX cable, the Macaron cable adopts more softer material thus you may get a confortable experience. They are both durable and flexible due to the strict selection of the durable PP double-sleeves raw materials. This structure enables the cable to be protected from fracture. The coiled design allows the cables easily to integrate into a wide range of spaces

【USB-C Interface, USB 2.0/3.0 Compatible】For versatile connectivity, each one is equipped with a detachable aviator port to accommodate different connectors. Included in this run is a USB-C connector cable to fit modern boards

【Six Colors Choices】The cable is designed in various light colors, inspiring from the dessert Macaron, to match different keyboard trend: yellow, light green, blue, pink and etc. Epomaker custom Macaron cable adds more vibrant energy to your daily life and work. Get it to refresh your keyboard right away!

reviews.title: It looks so much cooler!
reviews.text: These are way cooler than the standard cords! Just look at them guys.

reviews.title: Beautiful cable, working perfectly
reviews.text: I've wanted a coiled cable for a while but they're all so expensive. Found this epomaker one and I've bought products from epomaker before and haven't had any issues, and since this cable was only $20 I figured I could risk it. I was even happier when I saw they had a lavender option, most places just have light pink but not lavender. This cable matches my keycaps perfectly. The coiled part lays flat right out of the box (I've seen a few from other companies where the coiled part kinda curls up, which I didnt want), the cable feels nice and sturdy and good quality, and the metal connector also feels nice and heavy. I also like that this is a woven cable and not just plastic/rubber wrapped. Had no issues with the keyboard working after plugging in the cable. Absolutely worth the $20, I might buy the light pink one to match my other keyboards.

title: KINESIS USB-A Freestyle Pro Quiet Ergonomic Split Mechanical Keyboard (Cherry MX Silent Red Switches)
features: AWARD-WINNING SPLIT DESIGN: Advanced ergonomics for all body types and preferences via adjustable split, splay, and tent (tenting accessories sold separately). New cable storage compartment supports up to 20" of separation when you need it.

GENUINE CHERRY MECHANICAL SWITCHES FOR PREMIUM TYPING FEEL AND PERFORMANCE WITH LESS NOISE: Cherry MX "Silent" red mechanical switches feature a low activation force, linear feel and are rated for 50M key presses for unmatched durability.

FULL ONBOARD PROGRAMMABILITY WITH THE DRIVERLESS SMARTSET ENGINE: 9 custom layouts, 8 office hotkeys, on-the-fly macro recording, one-touch key remapping, pre-configured Mac and Dvorak layouts, settings saved to keyboards 4MB flash memory, and the SmartSet App GUI for Windows & Mac.
PLUG-AND-PLAY WITH ALL MAJOR OPERATING SYSTEMS: Windows 7-10, Mac OS X, Linux, Chrome.
2 YEAR MANUFACTURER'S WARRANTY: Buy with confidence knowing that Kinesis engineers all our products in the USA to the highest standards and stands behind them 100 percent since 1992.

reviews.title: Good, but could be better
reviews.text:
I use this with my MacBook Pro. I have brutal pain in my hands, wrists and forearms from osteoarthritis, tendon-patchy and tendonitis; caused from 19 years of being at my computer and holding my phone up for blogging.

This keyboard has made things so much better, however... I needed the keyboard to have the lift without the wrist pads which are agony for my hands. Only, the tent system is made to include all pieces; the lift and the wrist pads. So, I have to use the lift pieces without the wrist pads which leaves a chunk of awkward plastic sticking straight out from the keyboard; something I am constantly catching. It's frustrating to no end.

I am now looking for something I can simply glue to the inner, underside of the keyboard that will add the lift but leave the front open.

I'm also disappointed with the price. Come on now. It's sooooo overpriced. It's only a matter of time before another brand comes out with a more reasonable one- but in the meantime, I feel like they are taking advantage.

In the end, this is far better than nothing. It definitely places your hands in a more ergonomic position, and is better than a flat keyboard or laptop touchpad setup.

reviews.title: Great keyboard for ergonomics
reviews.text: The split keyboard layout has been great for my shoulders and arms.  It took a couple of weeks for me to get used to the layout but the extra functions offered on the edges of the keyboard are very helpful.  Being able to replace the key caps is huge.  I really love this keyboard.

reviews.title: Quality material, but as wide as TKL keyboards
reviews.text:
I wish the length of the cable was longer, and that a longer cable can be purchased separately.
For the price, the tenting should be standard, but it is a separate purchase. And, without them, this keyboard isn't as great.

I purchased the tenting separately, and it really helps with ergonomics when typing. But, when gaming it tends to push my hands side and down, so it's better to use a separate palm support.

I was surprised that when the keyboard is laid out, it is as wide as a ten keyless keyboard. I was hoping that it would be shorter, so that the mouse has more room. It's fine for work or just typing. For gaming, I'll put the right section farther up. Or, I'll put it above the keyboard tray, so I still have access to the keys when needed. That frees up a lot of room for the mouse.

The quality of the keyboard is great. Everything feels solid. The keys feel wonderful. And, the ergonomics is great for typing.
The tech support was friendly and helpful. I had a bit of a problem early on with customizing the keys, and their tech support promptly helped me.

reviews.title: Great keyboard
reviews.text: Nice quiet keyboard with a great feel.  I got the tenting and wrist rest kit.  It took a little bit to get used to the angle but I'm really enjoying it and have already programmed some of the keys to do routine things for me.

reviews.title: Perfect balance of ergonomics and key comfort.
reviews.text:
I have tried all the Kinesis keyboards with the exception of the 400$ console setup. I have chosen this one as the best overall experience, specifically paired with the VIP3Pro tent and wrist accessory. The keys on this keyboard have a much more consistent and pleasant feeling as compared to their membrane offerings as well as their silent switch option. Those keyboards are fantastic if you are not already addicted to the feeling of mechanical keys. However if you are searching for the ultimate in keyboard experiences for daily use, I heavily and without reservation recommend this keyboard set. The keyboard may initially seem expensive when compared to other ergonomic keyboards that do not split or offer the tenting/wrist rests, however having tried many different curved and non split ergonomic options from Microsoft, Logitech, and many others, I can say without a doubt that the optimal configuration requires the keyboard to be split. If you need to have a number pad like I do, I suggest any simple number key option, they'll fit nicely between the two halves and do not interfere at all. I plan to purchase a second keyboard for my WFH setup to compliment my work setup. I don't want to go back to a standard keyboard and you won't either after using it.

This review was not endorsed, sponsored, or influenced by any 3rd party, I am simply that overjoyed with the product.

title: YMDK GH60 GK61 RGB Hot Swap Independent Driver Tyce-C ANSI Mechanical Keyboard DIY kit Plastic Case CNC Aluminum Case Plate PCB (CNC Alu Cyan)
description: Package Included Case + Feet + Screws + GK61 Hot Swap PCB + Alu Silver Plate + USB Cable + Switch Removal Tool ​ Details The alu keyboard case is made of CNC aluminum sand Blast and then anodized.The plastic case is ABS amoulded. The chipset is MCU 32 bit,8M Flash,3 pcs 256*256*256 RGB drive to support full 16.8 million RGB. PCB has RGB presoldered,it's under per switch.PCB RGB can support by music. There is no switches in the kit,you can order Cherry Gateron Kailh SMD Box switches etc yourself.Just put in switches in is ok,no need soldering again. PCB is programmable with the software GK6x Plus,can download in official sitejikedingzhi dot com
features: Package Included:Case + Feet + Screws + GK61 Hot Swap PCB + Alu Plate + USB Cable + Switch Removal Tool
Pls note:The PCB is fully programmable with GK software,the Fn key is the last key at the bottom and can't change place.
The chipset is MCU 32 bit,8M Flash,3 pcs 256*256*256 RGB drive to support full 16.8 million RGB.
PCB has RGB presoldered,it's under per switch.PCB RGB can support by music.
There is no switches in the kit,you can order Cherry Gateron Kailh SMD Box switches etc yourself.Just put in switches in PCB is ok,no need soldering again.

reviews.title: Great pre-solder build. Comes with two cherry mx blacks and the Bluetooth is tricky.
reviews.text: Very good build quality overall. It came with two cherry mx black switches for the space bar and the right control keys which is a huge bonus. I purchased the Bluetooth white pcb which is very well built but the Bluetooth is very tricky. I have not been able to set it up yet even when I try to do the “fn+zxc” keys to setup for pairing it doesn’t work. Other than that it was a great purchase for the price and I will eventually figure out the Bluetooth.

reviews.title: Awesome first kit
reviews.text: First I want to state why I removed 1 star. I was missing 2 tiny screws that are easy to replace. However, the seller was very quick to reply and gave me all the details I needed to replace it. They even offered to fix the issue for me. But I am ok doing it myself. This is my first board build and I used Kahil Box Navy blues. The board feels amazing and well put together. Great first kit to get started. Can't wait to build my next board and get some more parts to form YMDK. I would choose the faster shipping if you are like me and hate waiting. I did get the board quicker than the projected date.

reviews.title: Great Kit for the money.
reviews.text:
this is a really great for the money. fit and finish is really pretty good, not great, but real good. the overall design is great, and the implementation is good. the clip in stabilizers are "lacking" but thats not a serious knock on the kit.

It is a good kit for the money for sure.

reviews.title: Awesome
reviews.text:
Worked out for the kids.
They liked it .

reviews.title: EASY TO MOD BAREBONES KIT
reviews.text:
Amazing little 60% board to mod.

I bought some switches, and installed them no problem.

Stabilizers are not pre lubed

Mods listed below

1.Tape mod on bottom of PCB
2.pennies on the bottom of the inside of the case
3.EVA foam inside case

Sounds amazing with lubed switches.

title: DROP + Matt3o MT3 Susuwatari Custom Keycap Set, ABS Hi-Profile Keycaps, Doubleshot Legends, Cherry MX Compatible Set for Ortholinear Mechanical Keyboards (Ortholinear)
features: A GROUNDBREAKING PROFILE, BACK FOR MORE: Sculpted in Hi-Profile, Matt3o’s groundbreaking MT3 /dev/tty custom keycap set changed the way we type.

FROM THE IBM TERMINAL TO THE DESKTOP: Based on the profile form the iconic IBM beamspring terminals of the 1970s, this custom set is fully sculpted in Hi-Profile. From its heavily slanted design to the scooped, contoured tops of each cap, the set recreates the feel of vintage keyboards.

DOUBLESHOT LEGENDS: As opposed to a dye-sublimiation process, these doubleshot legends are permanently molded inside the keycap, thus becoming an integral part of it. This ensures reliable legends and continuity throughout the set.
ORTHOLINEAR: For ortho keyboards like the Drop Planck, Preonic, and other boards with similar layouts.
SPECS: Produced by Drop and designed by Matt3o, ABS Hi-Profile MT3 keycaps, Doubleshot legends, MX compatible, and Fully sculpted based on IBM beamspring keycaps.

reviews.title: Absolute necessity on ortholinear boards
reviews.text:
These keycaps are thick, double shot so they will never fade, and sculpted. With ortholinear keyboards, these keycaps really help adjusting to smaller modifer key size and different key layout.

Pros:
1. Thick: This helps lower the pitch of the sound, which makes it sound quieter.
2. Doubleshot ABS: No wearing out the legends. The font is also fairly thick, making it much more legible than some other keycaps.
3. MT3 profile: The sculpted profile really makes it very comfortable to use and helps reduce hitting the wrong keys. No sharp edges due to the spherical shape. Very enjoyable for typing. The profile is much higher though so that could be an issue for some people.
4. Multiple profile options for certain keys: For example, the Ctrl and Esc keycs come in profiles that would allow it to be the top row or 3rd row. Space bar comes as two 1u, two 1.5u, and one 2u.
5. Storage tray and box: This seems silly to put as a pro but the tray and box it comes with make it easy to store unused or keycaps it replaced. Other Keycaps come in a zip lock bag that will just be a jumbled mess.

Cons:
1. Price. This is really expensive for a set of keycaps but this is the normal price for any specialty keycaps. It is what it is.
2. Limitation of scultped profile: Putting key profiles meant for row 1 in row 3 feels awkward so you might end you designing your keymap partially based on keycap profiles. Also might end up rotating some keycaps like row 5 Raise or Lower layer keycaps to make it more comfortable.
3. ABS option only. The PBT version is in white only. So if you prefer the color with the material that it isn't made it, then you're out of luck.
4. Limited to buying sets if you need extra keys: If you need more keys, you have to order a set. I would like to have more 1.5u blank keycaps but don't want to buy multiple sets just for the few pieces I want.

reviews.title: I like them
reviews.text:
These are my daily drivers, I use them on a Drop CTRL with Glorious Panda switches. Initially I thought the MT3 profile was disorienting, but after using them for a while, I am quite fond of it. I acknowledge that not everyone will like this profile though, and that should be considered if you are buying this product (or any like it). If you can find an MT3 set to try first before dumping about two hundred dollars on keycaps, it would be a **very good** idea. Other things go without saying, the thickness is very good the weight on my switches feels good and not like its making keys too easy to press or anything. I really like the theme, but all of that considered, I bought this specifically for the VI keys (hjkl with arrows) because I thought they looked super cool.

The price for these keycaps is a good deterrent for most people, for good reason. However this high of a price is pretty standard for high quality keycaps, as well as the Drop.com-exclusive MT3 profile.

This leads me to the value for money, its expensive and definitely should only be bought if you have a hankering to spend money on some expensive keycaps or you really really like having a specific row of keycaps.
Brightness is a 1 star because they're not shine-through keycaps, as they should be.
Gaming is a 4 star because, like, Sure? nothing about these keycaps keep you from gaming.

reviews.title: Best 3rd party caps I've ever owned.
reviews.text:
I really liked the esthetics of these key caps because I was looking for something which resembled "Old Nasa" feel, and these were the best I could find. The ergonomics were also great and everything has a very nice and snug fit, unlike the original keycaps that came with my CTRL keyboard.

I only wish that the "additional" buttons came with the normal key caps for the money I spent on the original "package". $30 for about 10 extra keys I can use on my ten keyless is VERY steep.

reviews.title: Best keycap set
reviews.text: I love this set. Worth every penny.

reviews.title: Absolutely gorgeous
reviews.text: Wonderful keycaps , goes great on my Nyquist Keyboard, looks professional yet custom, I highly recommend.

title: IDOBAO 10pcs MA Profile Keycaps 1u Ball Shape for Mechanical Keyboard DIY Blank Keycaps (Blue)

reviews.title: Good quality
reviews.text: Good quality and even better sound. Slightly pricey for the quantity but very much worth it.

title: Kraken Coiled Keyboard Cable - Artisan Coiled USB C Cable with Aviator Connector for Mechanical Gaming Keyboards (Pure White)
features: Premium Artisan Designs in colors to fit every gaming setup!
Heavy Duty 5 Pin Aviator Connector (Easy to connect, and looks AMAZING)
Double Sleeved for enhanced looks and durability!
5 Feet long to reach your keyboard anywhere in your setup

reviews.title: Excellent quality cable and connectors, but color is more purple than blue
reviews.text:
Update: Oddly enough, this did not work with a Drop Alt keyboard. It worked with a few other keyboards just fine though. Just a word of warning for anyone pairing this cable with a Drop Alt (or maybe any other Drop keyboard).

This is the third coiled cable I have ordered, and it's definitely the best quality overall. The cable itself is thick and good quality. The coil is a bit firm, but nothing too unusual. The intermediate connector is all metal and heavy. Both sides of the intermediate connector can be unthreaded: The side going to the USB-C connector, and the side going to the USB connector.

One interesting thing I noticed was that the end connectors have a color matched almost shrink wrap thickness strain protection around them. The USB side is the most noticeable, and the USB-C side is a bit thicker. As long as there is enough strain protection, I actually prefer the less bulky end connections.

The only nit is the color. Even though it looked dark purplish in the product listing, I was hoping that it would be more blue in real life. It's not, it's an attractive dark purple. I would assume the color represented in the product listing is accurate, in case you're wondering if there is any color variation from the listing photos to real life.

title: SNAP 75% Mechanical Keyboard Kit (White)
description: A stylish, 75% split The SNAP is a 75% split mechanical keyboard kit with support for 2 rotary encoders, 2 OLED displays, speaker, and RGB underglow. We took all the best parts of the NIBBLE and added some of the most popular requests: true split shift/space/backspace. The SNAP is configurable, programmable, and easily assembled. 100% through hole design The SNAP uses a Pro Micro MCU instead of a discrete micro-controller chip. It offers the fun and aesthetic of a kit without having to worry about soldering the difficult bits like USB connectors. All required parts diodes, caps, and ICs) are through-hole, meaning they're easy and forgiving to solder. underglow LEDs are surface-mount Multiple Layout Options Use the SNAP in split mode, or snap it whoa, get it?) together for a more traditional layout. Strong internal magnets hold the two sides together and magical spring-loaded pogo pin contacts mean there's no need for a TRRS cable when the two sides are attached. The SNAP supports many configurations and layouts: Split space/backspace, split shift, ISO enter, and more. Since it runs QMK firmware and uses VIA, it's easy to change keymaps, program macros, and customize functionality. RGB underglow ready 10x optional bottom-mounted SMT LEDs shine nicely through the acrylic support material. Included Top & bottom PCB Acrylic supports M2 standoffs & screws Diodes, ICs, decoupling caps, reset switch RGB underglow, speaker, magnets, and pogo pins Rotary encoder & color-matched knob TRRS cable and mini bumpons Not included 2x Bit-Cs or Pro Micro Compatible MCU MX-compatible switches & keycaps PCB-mount stabilizers NOTE: This is a kit which requires assembly and soldering! Bit-Cs, switches, keycaps, and other accessories are sold separately
features: The SNAP is a high-quality split 75% keyboard kit with tons of room for customization
Use the SNAP together, or separate! It snaps together for a traditional 75% layout or pulls apart for use as a split board
Built around a Pro Micro, rather than a discrete microcontroller. This saves assembly time, and also reduces the build difficulty
All required parts (diodes, caps, and ICs) are through-hole, meaning they're easy and forgiving to solder

RGB underglow ready: 10x optional bottom-mounted SMT LEDs shine nicely through the acrylic support material. RGB underglow is supported natively in QMK, allowing for easy programming and configuration

reviews.title: Not for the faint of heart
reviews.text:
This kit does take a lot of work so I would not recommend it as a first build, or for someone looking for a quick build.

It is also a fairly unique one of a kind kit. I don't know of other split 75% keyboards with pro micro support that also have great documentation, support and community. If you are willing to put in the time you will be rewarded with a unique keyboard, and the kit really is fun to put together for experienced builders.

I would knock down half star as I encountered some issues with the fitting of the acrylic plate, specifically in these areas:
1. acrylic plate fitting at the keyboard split edge, had to use an exacto knife
2. acrylic plate fitting with bottom screws of the PCB mount stabilizers, had to make the holes wider
3. snap magnets did not fit, gave up trying to fit these
4. components pin through the solder joints interfered with acrylic, this is expected and is easily fixable by trimming them down.

Apart from that everything worked as expected. Firmware worked on first try and I was easily able to get it working with VIA.

title: Hyekit Custom Gaming Keyboard - Alice Layout 66 Keys - Support QMK/Via Programmable Key - Gasket Structure - USB C Wired Mechanical Keyboard Kit - RGB Hot Swappable Switches & Keycaps - Acrylic Shell
description:
Note:
This link only sells a kit, not a finished keyboard, you need to assemble it yourself, the kit does not include switches, keycaps, data cables, or stabilizers.
About This Item
1.FancyAlice66 keyboard kit Alice is equipped with 66 keys, ergonomic, yes typing is more comfortable.  2. Hot-swappable PCB uses Kailh hot-swappable socket and supports 3-pin and 5-pin mechanical switches.  3. 32U4 chip master, support QMK/VIA, kit supports QMK/VIA, more customized, you can use it to modify key functions and customize more functions.  4. South-facing RGB Backlit has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight makes the keyboard look more attractive.  5.Gasket Mount, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, which makes the keyboard more comfortable and soft to type, and the typing sound is more pleasant.  6. Acrylic stacking shell, the texture is very good, enjoy the fun of custom assembling the shell.  7. Wired mode, support USB-C, typing response is faster, Type-c interface adapts to more data lines.  8. The kit needs to be assembled by yourself. First, ensure that you have a certain ability to assemble. You can refer to our assembly instruction video for assembly.  9.Support PCB Mounted and Plate Mounted stabilizers, we recommend you to use PCB Mounted stabilizers.
Package includes:
Keyboard case*1  PCB*1  Quiet sandwich cotton*1  Switch pad*1  Assemble some parts
features: 【Package Included】Keyboard case*1, PCB*1, Quiet sandwich cotton*1, Switch pad*1, Assemble some parts. No USB cables, switches, stabilizer, and keycaps. (Note, The kit needs to be assembled by yourself. you ensure that you have a certain ability to assemble. You can refer to our assembly instruction video for assembly. Let's enjoy the process of assembling the keyboard and the great sense of achievement brought by the successful assembly.)

【Custom Gaming Keyboard】Hot-swappable PCB uses Kailh hot-swappable socket and supports 3-pin and 5-pin mechanical switches. Support PCB Mounted and Plate Mounted stabilizers, we recommend you to use PCB Mounted stabilizers. 32U4 chip master, support QMK/VIA, more customized, you can use it to modify key functions and customize more functions. Wired mode, supports USB-C, typing response is faster.

【Alice Layout】The Alice layout considers the natural input angle of the wrist from the perspective of ergonomics. When using the conventional arrangement keyboard for continuous high-intensity input work, the unnatural bending of the wrist will easily lead to hand fatigue, and the Alice layout can solve this problem very well, it takes into account the integrity of the keyboard and the relatively relaxed input state of the wrist.

【South-facing RGB Backlit】Alice66 has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight, the RBG Backlight effect is very bright even in the daytime. The lower lamp position is designed so that the light will not be blocked by the switch. The fully transparent bottom cover allows the 16 bottom lights to display their backlight effects without obstruction, making the keyboard look more attractive.

【Acrylic Gasket Structure】Alice66 is designed with Gasket structure, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, the sound is pure, and the feel is soft. This wonderful feel can make the typing process very pleasant and full of fun.

reviews.title: Sounds great with no mods
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:58cc2520e806ff1428542a1df7456920]] Assembly was pretty easy for this DIY kit. It even sounds great without any modifications. I decided to put poron switch pads on the pcb since the foam it came with did not line up properly. The feet that comes with this kit is too aggressive for my liking. I decided to remove it and put clear rubber bumpons

Did not come with stabilizers. I was hoping it does at this price point. Speaking of which, this kit can be had for cheaper on other retailers. But ordering from Amazon gave me more confidence.

My copy came with cracked and scuffed case. I messaged the seller directly and they were able to help and send me a new case promptly. They also helped with VIA setup on this keyboard.

Pros:
- easy assembly
- VIA support PCB
- great sound with little to no modifications
- south facing, per key RGB
- under glow lighting
- A+ great customer support from seller

Cons:
- price seems higher compared to other websites
- inconsistent craftsmanship
- no stabilizers included
- front of the keyboard can be wobbly with the aggressive feet riser installed

(Keycaps: MT3 2048; Switches: WS Aurora Clears)

title: Matcha Keycaps Set 124 Keys XDA PBT Keycaps Dye Sublimation Fit for 61/64/87/104/108 Cherry Mx Switches Mechanical Keyboard

reviews.title: Nice keycaps
reviews.text: Fits my Anne Pro case without issue. Nice colors and easy to type with.

reviews.text: This is a clone set as far as I'm aware, but I don't mind that. The arrows are cuter than the "original" Matcha set that can be found for the same price. These feel great, sound great, look great. I couldn't be happier!

reviews.title: these are sweeeet

reviews.title: Lovely green color

title: Akko Keycaps Set, Black&Bronze Theme 158-Key ASA Profile PBT Double-Shot Keycap Set with Black Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards with Collection Box
features: BLACK&BRONZE THEME – The combination with classic black, gray and dark bronze,　the Black&Bronze keycaps brings heavy sense of history. The bronze charactors shine on the dark keycaps let us fantasy the bronze gleams in the dark night through thick fog.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the 158-key keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including but not limited to 61-key(60%), 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%) and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure switches including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh etc. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Some of the best keycaps under $100
reviews.text:
These keycaps are fantastic. The quality of material is noticeable right when you open the package, and the layout compatibility is uncomparable. For about $60 you get keycaps that work with the following layouts:

Full Size
1800
96%
75%
65%
60%
40% (non ortholinear)

The only layouts that aren't supported are ortholinear (though you might be able to make something work) and split (no split spacebar here). 60% with arrows (the 64-key) also is not supported as there is no 2u shift.

The colors are fantastic, the material feels very nice, and the cap profile is just incredibly comfortable to type on. In fact, it's so comfortable the only caps that I think are nicer are Drop's MT3 which cost double and don't come with as much layout variation without buying extra kits.

This specific set, Dracula's Castle, also comes with custom novelites and two different colors worth of compatibility (crimson and black) for a lot of the keys to make your board look exactly the way you want.

I'd highly recommend anything in ASA profile. The Cherry profile is good too, but not s comfortable for me to type on personally.

reviews.title: So amazing for the price
reviews.text: They looks so good for the price, Akko does it again!

reviews.title: Great quality double shot pbt keycaps.
Pretty Great Set with an Interesting Profile
reviews.text: This set is pretty nice, unlike most of Akko's keycap sets this set doesn't directly rip off color ways from existing GMK or EBPT sets, it comes close to something like SA Bliss but it adds black mods so it's not exactly the same.  The keycaps themselves are fairly nice and the font of the legends are a lot more consistent than their cherry profile sets. The packing is nice albeit a little frustrating as you pretty much need to use a keycap puller to remove the keycaps. The ASA profile is what I found the most interesting, if I had to compare it to anything I would actually compare it to OEM profile but slightly taller with rounded edges than something SA profile, The sculpting is a lot more derivative of OEM than SA in my opinion. The typing feel is pretty great it is very reminiscent of OEM profile so if you are used to OEM profile you will be right at home. The sound is more clacky than thocky at least in my NK65 with Gateron Cap Gold Switches. Overall the keycaps are nice and the ASA profile is a pleasant surprise coming from someone that uses both SA and OEM profile fairly regularly.

reviews.title: Good quality, Vivid colors, Nice sounding, Great value
reviews.text:
Akko Los Angeles 158-Key ASA Profile PBT Double-Shot Full Keycap Set for Mechanical Keyboards with Collection Box

I really love the profile of these keycaps. I have a hard time with whether to call this company a knock off company or not. They do have their own color combos and make combos that other companies do for a lot cheaper. Are color combos and fonts really proprietary? I'm not sure and eventually there will be no more combination of colors that one company will be able to claim as only their colors. I do think that Akko make really good keycaps and you get a lot for your money. So that's my two cents.

title: Lomiluskr K680T Wireless 60% Mechanical Keyboard, Ultra-Compact Bluetooth/Wired Dual Mode Keyboard, White Backlight, Compatible for Multi-Device Connection (Black Switch, Black)
features: 【68 Key Mini and Compact Layout】60% Layout 68Keys Mechanical Keyboard. As a compact mechanical keyboard, while minimize the size to 13.4×4.25×1.4inch, we maintain the Mac key position and commonly used keys for games. This size saves lots of desktop space, giving your right hand and mouse more space, whether you are working, gaming or daily use, it will perform perfectly. Small in the appearance but big inside

【Bluetooth Wireless/Wired Mode】Designed with Bluetooth mode and wired dual mode, you have more choices. Bluetooth3.0 is stable and strong enough to ensure 10 meters transmission distance. It can connect up to 3 devices at the same time, simply press FN+Q/W/E to exchange between devices, easy to use and suitable for both work and game. Moreover, the wired mode will also charge the keyboard at the same time, a cool function and time-saver indeed

【1000mAh Built-in Large Capacity Battery】N-Key Rollover and Built-in 1000mAh Battery. This model is programmed to be N-key rollover in wired mode. The keys won’t conflict with each other. Save you from trouble while playing a game. Built-in large capacity battery ensures long time use without falling behind

【18 Light Effects】The letters are made to shine-through if the backlight is on. Simple backlight but you have got up to 18 light effects, classic and neat (flashing, static or breathing, etc.) use FN+→/←/↑/↓ to choose the ideal mode, freely adjust the brightness and light speed.

【Professional Mechanical Switches】Mechanical Switches and Professional Chip. This model is equipped with the custom chip for fast and lossless response. The 68 keys are using the real mechanical switches for ultimate tapping experience. Lightweight, weak tactile and audible feedback, these switches are the perfect combination of typing and gaming, more than 50 million click tests for excellent durability.

reviews.title: Won't pair with M1 Macbook Air, works well with Dell XPS 9370 and Acer Triton Predator
reviews.text:
For windows users this keyboard is great. The brown switches feel somewhat similar to higher end cherry brown switches and I had no complaints with the build quality. There are various lighting modes (my favorite is the single keystroke light trail).

I was slightly disappointed with the Bluetooth not working on my M1 Macbook Air but I had no other issues with various windows laptops. There is some latency noticeable when waking from sleep but it's not too big of a deal. For gamers it might be though so you'll want to use it wired.

Overall it's a good deal for a mechanical 60% bt keyboard as long as you're not expecting premium switches or bt 5.0 connectivity.

reviews.title: Compact But Has Good Weight, Keys Are Perfect
reviews.text:
I opted for the "brown" keys and I must say that they feel and respond perfectly. Just the right amount of "clack" and just the right amount of resistance. The keys are normal width but the footprint is compact so you don't have to sacrifice any typing comfort for the portability or the ability to work in small spaces. The keyboard itself is very weighty and between its heft and rubber feet it's pleasingly very stable - won't slide around on the desk. The ability to use either USB or Bluetooth makes this thing incredibly versatile. Thankfully it's USB-C so you don't have to worry about plug alignment, and the included USB cable is very good quality (braided nylon, strain relief) and length. The backlighting is decent, though not terribly even so some of the lettering doesn't look right in the dark.

One big downside is the lack of dedicated Function keys (F1-F12). This keyboard takes a cue from laptop keyboard design and introduces an "Fn" key which you have to hold in to change the function of normal keys to specialty operations, but instead of controlling volume or screen brightness, the "Fn" key is required to activate bluetooth pairing, an feature that acts sort of like Caps Lock but for function keys and of course the "F" keys. The problem I ran into with this configuration is that the F1-F12 keys weren't being recognized with some devices. I actually bought this specifically to use with a MiSTer FPGA device which supports standard USB keyboards and requires the F keys for certain basic functions.

According to the instructions, the combination "Fn + 1" should look like an "F1" key press but it did not. After poking at it for some time I discovered an undocumented (as in it's not in the instructions anywhere) feature which subtly changes the keyboard's operation. The "A" and "S" keys have an additional label "Os1" and "Os2" respectively. Holding "Fn + A" or "Fn + S" makes the keyboard backlight flash (which it does whenever you've changed some function of the keyboard itself). With the keyboard set to "Os2" (Fn + S) the "Fn + 1" combination DOES send "F1" but, this arrangement also confusingly swaps the "Window" key and the left "Alt" key.

Bottom Line ---
Pros:
- Heavy
- Quality keys, great feel
- Bluetooth & USB
- USB is USB-C and comes with a good cord
- Easy to plug in and use

Neutral Observations:
- Backlight is uneven

Cons:
- Instructions are incomplete
- Not having dedicated F1-F12 keys is problematic for gaming

title: VELOCIFIRE Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard Mini, 78-Key Compact Ergonomic, Outemu Brown Switches Backlit and Double-Shot ABS Keycaps for Copywriter, Typist and Programmer
features: MUST HAVE- Velocifire mechanical keyboard is designed for copywriter, programmer, editors who spend many hours typing every day. You will get better at typing speed and accuracy through Velocifire mechanical keyboards.

Durable Brown Switch- Low-noise switches have a tactile bump for feedback with every key-press. It is much QUIETER than Blue Switch but LOUDER than a regular Membrane keyboard. If you never used a mechanical keyboard before, please think about whether you can accept its sound first.

Compact Design- The compact mechanical keyboard helps save desktop space.Tiny size of compact keyboard makes it possible to share room with a mouse. Users like to have to mouse close to keyboard, since that will be easier to reach.It also serves as a accessory of laptop.

ANTI-GHOSTING - This is sometime used in place of Key Rollover, but the terminology is incorrect since ghosting is a different (and rare) issue. You will need to do more research to see what key rollover technology is being used. Ghosting is when two or more keys are pressed and a key that was not pressed registers instead.
Money Back -- Velocifire offers a 30 days money back. We always serve you after the sale!

reviews.title: The way to go

reviews.text:
This keyboard is a huge bargain, but the layout is not right. The bottom third and fourth rows don't match what you'd expect from a keyboard.

If you don't touch-type, then this keyboard is great. If you do, it's not so great.

reviews.title: Sounds horrendous
reviews.text: So I should start off to say the build quality is good and the switches are nice. My complaint is the noise that is made when bottoming the keys out on the aluminum top. It makes a loud and high pitched ringing noise that is far from pleasant. The small form factor would make it great for gaming however the sound when hitting the keys are is not good. Ultimately it could have been a nice keyboard but it fell short.

reviews.title: LOVE IT
reviews.text: I absolutely 💯 ♥️ love this keyboard! It reminds me of typing class in high school. If you want nostalgia this is your product. It does make a very satisfying loud clicking when typing so do not buy if you need something quiet. Now my sister wants one too!

reviews.title: I am in love
reviews.text: I never thought I can fall in love with a keyboard! This keyboard is esthetically beautiful. I love this clicking sound…not quite like the real typewriter but kinda close:)  Light effects make typing even more fun

reviews.title: Beautiful keyboard
reviews.text: The keyboard is absolutely unique and stunning, I just received it yesterday so I have not typed on it very much but it seems pretty heavy duty and the back lighting is awesome especially with bad eyesight, The return arm regulates the lights to make it very smooth to adjust, The typewriter knobs actually do the volume on your computer so all in all I do love the keyboard, The wireless feature actually was pretty easy to set up just read the instructions carefully!  I have never spent more than $40 on a keyboard and wireless mouse but I have to tell you well worth it.

reviews.title: nice build. aesthetically interesting looking.
reviews.text: I tried to return this thinking it has connectivity issues but troubleshot my way to realize it was OS messin' with me.  Glad I gave it a another whirl because it's really nice!  one thing it does do is the lower keys next to the space button are all the same height and my fat lower palm always hits them and sends me to the beginning of the sentence I'm writing.  Annoying and somewhat dangerous for my line of work but I solved it by putting elevation risers at the front raising it approximately another 8-10 degrees.  Would have been nice if it had elevation standoffs as standard equipment.  bluetooth on this is fantastic and responsive.

reviews.title: Spacebars do not fit well
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:14b0c07be1bf5b90e20f388d925ce8c8]] The keycap set is great in terms of design, modifier key size options, and key legend for quality for price. Fitting for all keys is great, excepts for the large spacebars.
The space bars (both 6.25u and 7u) in my set don’t work smoothly and somehow get the stabs stuck (two different kinds of stabs as well). I tried them on two keyboards (one with 7u and other with 6.25u) and only these spacebars showed problems and the other spacebars I have do not show this problem. I am not sure really whats causing the space bar issue, maybe those pbt space keycaps are slightly bent (as I have read about) but visually they look okay.

reviews.title: Good Quality for the $ - Fits Cherry MX Switches
reviews.text:
This fit my Ducky mechanical keyboard without issue. They seem to be well made and fit tightly in my cherry MX switches. A couple observations, not sure it’s a positive or a negative but a point of observation:

1) They do not come with media key replacements. The blue ones you see in my image are from my original key caps

2) The height of the keys are slightly shorter than my Ducky OEMs. While initially this was a possible concern for me, having typed with the new keys for a couple weeks, seems to not impact typing at all.

3) The blue caps that came with the replacements are not the ones you see in my photo (Enter key). They have a slightly yellow / blue hue to them vs. the Ducky’s I’m using. Wanted my enter key to match the rest of the blue media keys so I mixed and matched. Some may like the blue that comes with the set, I just preferred my original blue keys color.

For the price, they fit the bill, can’t complain. We’ll see long term how the printing of the keys holds up / how quickly they wear down.

Wish list:

- Caps lock key had the word ‘Caps’ on it
- Windows button keys had something besides “Super”.

Overall good purchase.

reviews.title: I liked it very much until it started disconnecting
reviews.text:
I've only had it for a week and the type C end moves and it disconnects from my keyboard. it's not a lot but enough to power off my key board.

But, aside from that little piece of failure, i really like the sturdiness and the quality of the work on it. It's also at a great price. That's why i'm not returning this item, it's actually really good and as long as the cable is held in place it works great. Maybe it's my WFH wonky setup. I would buy more if i needed more cables, but I'm set for life with cables.

really nice cable, i would recommend to others.

reviews.title: Works great
reviews.text: Purchased for my son.  He says it works great.

reviews.title: Great upgrade for any Keyboard, best with custom rugged builds.
reviews.text: I wanted to get a substitute USB-C cable for my keyboard and found this listing. Was intrigued by the interchangeable inputs as well as the mechanical design. The price seemed steep, but the uses, ergonomics and possible durability of the cable seemed like a good investment. Mine came functional with an included "Kraken Keybaord" sticker, thank you! The only problem I found was that the USB-C male input was a tad long for my ANNE PRO Clone, a Dierya x Kemove DK61 60% Keyboard. It might be my keyboard's own build quality, or that maybe these cables are meant for more rugged custom boards. If I move my keyboard drastically it might disconnect, but luckily that rarely happens. I'm more than satisfied with the cable, more like enamored. I'd buy again for a future custom keyboard build for sure.

reviews.title: Great looks, less great feel
reviews.text: These are supposedly double shot ABS keycaps, and they are for sure double shot, but they have the smoothest texture I've ever felt on a keycap. Think painted ABS gaming keyboard keycaps, that smooth if not even smoother. They don't sound half bad. I think the stems could fit stabilizers a little better seeing as if I push a bit too hard on one side the other lifts up at times. I can't complain much further considering I paid $27 for these after a discount and you get what you pay for. Overall I'd say they're worth the money, but don't go expecting super high quality or anything.

reviews.title: What a Fantastic Bargain
reviews.text:
I don't normally write reviews, but I liked this set and decided to pull the trigger on a product that barely had any reviews. I bought this yesterday and installed the keys a day later. I put these keys on my Glorious Pro keyboard and am in love. Now my experience is limited to the Kinetic Labs Whale keycap set and a cheap knock off from the China Amazon that are in OEM height, so not a direct comparison. These keys have the same build quality and sound as the Kinetic Labs set at almost 1/3 of the price.

I'm not an expert but I did take a picture of the spacebars and it does appear that the blue version may be warped slightly or disfigured, I don't know. I took the picture incase it helped. Outside of that, to my untrained eye everything else looked perfect. Gorgeous set.

I liked the options this seller had in other keycap sets, so I used this set as a trial run and was not disappointed. I will be looking to order other colors in the future.

New edition support all 61 64 layout as the image show,you need to use the plate which support these layout at the same time to support the layout you prefer.

reviews.title: !!!!!Great PCB!!!!!!!!
reviews.text:
Used this for my Switch Couture Electrotype60 amazing PCB and pretty easy to solder, especially for an amateur solderer like me, definitely will be buying another one when I have another case to build. Fit perfectly, No problems soldering, and definitely came out perfect out of the box, though I don't know how to brighten up the LED nor change the modes, I'm completely fine with the RGB cycle. I'm Typing on my new keyboard as we speak and it works perfectly, no delays, no nothing, just perfect!!!!

(I can admit I'm kinda too last to do VIA so I'm just working with it lol)

title: TECWARE Phantom 87 Key Mechanical Keyboard, RGB led, Outemu Brown Switch
features: RGB BACKLIGHTING - Enhance your gaming experience with 18 pre-set configurations on the mechanical keyboard, or create a unique one to match your setup

OUTEMU SWITCHES - The gaming mechanical keyboard comes with Outemu mechanical switches for precise gaming and comfortable typing. Spare switches + keycap & switch remover tools are provided for easy replacement
FIBERGLASS PCB - Built with SMD LEDs and FR-4 fiberglass printed circuit board, this Tecware mechanical keyboard is able to withstand extended periods of intensive and hardcore gaming sessions

DOUBLE-SHOT KEYCAPS – Featuring an all-new font, the keycaps on this mechanical keyboard are double-shot ABS (dual-injection molding process) which guarantee high resistance against surface wear; markings that cannot be worn off as they are physically part of the keycaps

SPEED ADVANTAGE - With full N-key rollover and 1000Hz pooling rate, Tecware keyboards allow faster and smoother gaming experience. The depression of each key is detected individually, meaning all key presses will be registered, no matter how many keys you press down simultaneously

reviews.title: Great quality
reviews.text: Very bright lights. Click clack noise keys. Small the way I like it. Portable. Works great. Has usb cover cap too.

reviews.title: Excellent keyboard.
reviews.text: This is my first mechanical keyboard and I really love the quality of this keyboard. The LED's are bright and on keyboard LED controls are very useful as you don't have to depend on the software. The keyboard feels very premium and on par with any other expensive keyboard. I would highly recommend this to others looking for new mechanical keyboard.

reviews.title: the smaller one with the browns... i love it
reviews.text: ok the onboard RGB stuff is really why i bought this. Its not Razer RGB but for +/- 50 bucks? oh hell yea. The keys are great, if your used to others it might take a sec to get accustomed, but if you use it your fingers will get to know it real nice :) Solid thru and thru.

reviews.title: Yes

title: Glorious Aura V2 (Black) - PBT Pudding Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards - ANSI (US), ISO Compatible - Supports Full Size, TKL, 75%, 60% Layouts
features: ✅Glorious Aura Keycaps V2 : This redesigned 145 key pudding keycap layout is compatible with GMMK Regular/PRO and most keyboard sets
✅Comes in black or white colors
✅Standard OEM Profile: Glorious Aura Pudding Keycap Set features a standard OEM profile that is widely used over many mechanical keyboards
✅Doubleshot injection legends, backlight RGB led compatible (transparent legends and sides), comes with 1 extra Glorious ASCEND ESC key and original Aura ESC key
✅Thick, High-Quality PBT Plastic Keys : Glorious Aura Pudding Keycaps are made with quality material that is resistant to wear, friction, and solvents
✅Transparent Legends, With Transparent Sides : Glorious Aura Pudding Keycaps are designed to let more light through, making your keyboard's RGB lighting more vibrant

✅Updated font compared to Glorious Aura V1 and support for more mechanical keyboards, compatible with layouts with STANDARD BOTTOM ROW. Fully compatible with Glorious GMMK Mechanical Gaming Keyboards (KEYBOARD NOT INCLUDED), please check your keyboard layout for compatibility

reviews.title: Best keycaps I have ever used.
reviews.text:
Bought these for my GMMK pro.

I have owned several mechanical keyboards, but never bought a high-end set of keycaps. Unlike every other set I've ever used, these just seem to ignore dirt and grease. I can stuff my face with a big Mac and go straight to typing, and the caps stay completely clean. I don't know how that works but I won't question it.

Other keyboards require weekly wipe downs, I have never had to do it with these caps.

They're also noticeably built well. These are going to outlast my fingers by a hundred years, likely.

reviews.title: Legend doesn’t glow like other pics
reviews.text: For my Tofu65, I do like these pudding key caps, but I’m not sure why the legends on my key caps aren’t transparent. Everyone who posted reviews with pics show their legends glowing, so I’m not sure why mine are different. My other issue is I got several duplicative keys, and I’m missing a few. For example, I only have one home key and about 4 page down keys. So that’s slightly annoying. I’ve seen some others complain about this similar thing as well. But the feel and look of these key caps are quality. Slightly disappointed.

reviews.title: Great caps work as advertised
reviews.text: Works great

title: Metal Switch Opener GPL 205G0 0.35oz, Lubrication Oil Mechanical Keyboard Keycaps Aluminum Alloy Opener Instantly for Cherry mx Switches
description: As an indispensable tool for custom players, I think you will often use the switch opener, which can be placed on your workbench. Then this ultra-high-value and super-feeling opener will be your best choice, it can open any cherry switch, and the switch body similar to the cherry switch structure. It can turn on the switch in one second without any lag. We have tested and improved it several times. Compared with other switch openers, it has more personality, adds more surprising details, and is more smooth to use. This set includes a switch opener, as 4 Prong tool and a lubed brush. Compared to holding the switch by hand, I believe that the 4 Prong tool is a better choice for you. It can make your work process more professional and faster. The lubed Brush is slender and precise, and it can find exactly where you need lubrication. Product packaging:A Metal Switch Opener A 4 Stem Holder  A Lube Brush A GPL 205G0
features: 【GPL 205G0】It is enough to lubricate 300 switches. 205 grease can be used on satellite and balance bar.
【Switch Opener】This keyboard switch opener is made of aluminum alloy material, using anodizing process, matte texture, and never fading.
【Convenient】 It includes complete set of accessories, you can have all tools at one time.This is a perfect accessory set for those who like to mod their switches.
【Package Includes】1 pcs GPL 205G0, 1 pcs Metal Switch Opener, 1 pcs Stem Holder, 2 pcs Lube brushes.
【Warm Tips】This switch opener can only be used for cherry switch, temporarily not suitable for other switches.

reviews.title: Gamer kid loves this!
reviews.text: Bought as a gift for my gamer teen, and they love it! The kids are all into the clickity clack of keyboard sounds, and hers is so great now that her friends are asking her to clean and lube their keyboards and paying her to do it! Awesome product!

reviews.title: Almost everything you'll need to start fixing your keyboard.
reviews.text:
I was able to use this to make a 'cheap' Corsair board sound like a $300 board. The only other thing that would have made this a perfect kit is maybe a switch puller?

Definitely worth the price and the seller was quick to replace my missing items.

title: Akko Pink Keycaps Set, Clear Translucent 155-Key ASA Profile Keys with Mac Keycaps Set for Mechanical Keyboard
features: TRANSPARENT KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Clear keycap sets! Made by polycarbonate, the keycap is solid and translucent so that your switches and RGB array can shine through. Characters on the ASA Clear keycaps are processed through UV printing with wear-resistant and scratch-resistant tests, and are not easily wiped off.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the 155-key keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

MAC KEYCAPS & NOVELTY KEYS – The keycap set is designed for both Windows and Mac users, where Mac modifiers CMD/OPT are offered in different sizes. The transparent keycap set also features extra cute animal themed novelty keys for decoration.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: Impressed
reviews.text: Very good packaging and acceptable quality

reviews.title: Unique keys look cool with RGB. Some drawbacks.
reviews.text:
Pros:
- Beautifully and conveniently packaged and organized. Caps come on three trays with plastic film on them so that nothing will have shifted in transit. It's very easy to replace your caps and keep your old set save and organized in the trays, too. Simple to find any specialized keys that you may need in the trays.
- Print is easy to see and seems durable/high contrast. No funky fonts that are hard to read.
- Looks awesome with RGB.

Cons:

- There are no tactile nubs on the F and J keys to indicate homerow. As a touch typist, this was almost a dealbreaker.
- Color is way off from the images in the description. The images in the description show a clear white color to the keycaps. Along with the "clear/translucent" descriptors, I believed I was going to get more of a white/iced translucent look. Nope. The caps are basically colorless clear. They are smooth and glossy with no texture. I left some of the OG white keys on my keyboard to show the color clearly side by side. Not the look I was expecting from the images and description.

Good to know:

- I've never used ASA profile before, and generally prefer XDA, so these are a little taller than I'm used to on XDA, but more rounded (and, I think, more comfortable, than OEM. I had some lousy stock OEM caps on my old keyboard, and I do feel these caps are an improvement. They are sufficiently dense, and give me a slightly deeper, more muted sound from my keypresses with my brown switches, which I like, compared to the more plasticky/hollow caps that were original to my board.
- Despite having a glossy finish, I don't find these keys to be slippery--the slight curvature leads your fingertips toward the center of the keys easily and they're comfortable to use.
- I really hate that there are no homerow nubs. Who skips that? Come on.

reviews.title: No tactile bumps on F and J, amazing for backlighting
reviews.text:
I have the “White” color option in this set of keycaps from Akko. White is a bit misleading, the color is actually glossy and nearly translucent. This is actually what I wanted, I was looking all over for nearly clear keycaps, but I did want to point that out.

If you like maximum backlighting, this might be the set for you. I was using white pudding caps before, and I thought they let a lot of light shine through. The difference is night and day. These Akko caps show backlighting on all sides and the top of the cap. I’ve included a picture of a white pudding cap surrounded by the clear Akkos to show the difference.

I’m using the keycaps with Akko Vintage White (MX) switches. The keycaps fit well, but they took a bit of force to fit on the switches.

I am disappointed the F and J keys do not have ridges or bumps. I’m a touch typist and using an ergo board with 56 keys, I rely on those ridges to help me orient my hands. This set has so much variety that I wish Akko had included doubles of F and J or even blanks with the ridges.

Speaking of keycap variety: the set has some great 1u options! My current keyboard is an Iris Rev 6, all keys are 1u size. But most keycap sets don’t have 1u keycaps for Shift, Control, Command/Windows, and so on. This set does, which is a big help. I also like the cute animal keycaps, they add some fun to my board.

I believe the ASA height is original to Akko. They have a helpful height chart on their site, comparing ASA to OEM, JDA, OSA, and Cherry. I was using OEM before, and switching to ASA and the glossy feel of the keycaps took about two days of adjustment.
